Protein names: Cobalamin biosynthesis bifunctional protein CbiET [Includes: Cobalt-precorrin-7 C(5)-methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.289); Cobalt-precorrin-6B C(15)-methyltransferase (decarboxylating) (EC 2.1.1.196)]
Protein families: Precorrin methyltransferase family; Methyltransferase superfamily, Bacterial-type CbiT family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: In the N-terminal section; belongs to the precorrin methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000305}.; SIMILARITY: In the C-terminal section; belongs to the methyltransferase superfamily. Bacterial-type CbiT family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=Co-precorrin-7 + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = Co-precorrin-8X + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:34591, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:70791, ChEBI:CHEBI:70792; EC=2.1.1.289; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:23922391}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=Co-precorrin-6B + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = Co-precorrin-7 + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + CO2; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:36067, ChEBI:CHEBI:16526, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:70791, ChEBI:CHEBI:72780; EC=2.1.1.196; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:23922391};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Bifunctional enzyme that catalyzes the methylation of C-15 in cobalt-precorrin-6B followed by the decarboxylation of C-12 to form cobalt-precorrin-7, and then methylates cobalt-precorrin-7 at C-5 to form cobalt-precorrin-8. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:23922391}.

Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the precorrin methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000305}.

Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catalyzes the methylation of C-5 in cobalt-precorrin-7 to form cobalt-precorrin-8. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:16198574}.
